data_structures.SA: Survey Averaged (1m spatial resolution) struct array containing spatially-averaged survey point data (x,y,z elevation in UTM coords, substrate classification). One element per survey date. Fields: .File, .Datenum, .Source, .X, .Y, .Z, .Class, .Mopnum, .Bytes
data_structures.SG: Spatial Grid (interpolated survey) struct array containing gridded elevation data on 1m UTM grid for each survey. Bridges SA raw points to SM transect-based profiles. Fields: .File, .Datenum, .Source, .X, .Y, .Z, .Class, .Mopnum, similar to SA but gridded
data_structures.SM: Survey Morphology (profile-based) struct array containing cross-shore transect profiles and morphometric parameters extracted from SG grids. Fields: .X1D (xshore distance), .Z1Dtransect, .Z1Dmean, .Z1Dmedian, .Datenum, .Source, .File, .Mopnum, plus computed metrics like beach widths, slopes, volumes
data_structures.Survey: Master survey metadata struct array with global survey inventory. Fields: .File (full path), .Datenum (survey date), .Source (survey type: Gps, UTAir, Trk, TrkMR, AtvMR, DrnMR, USACE, CCC, USGS, KMair, iG8wheel, RTKdrone, etc), .Type, .Year, .Month, .Day, .MopStart, .MopEnd, .NearestMops (list of mops with data in file), .FileDatenum (file creation date), .Bytes
data_structures.GM: Global Mean struct containing statistics over all surveys for a Mop. Fields: .Mopnum, .X2D, .Y2D (global grid UTM coords), .Z2Dmin, .Z2Dmax, .Z2Dmean (mean elevation grid), .Z2Dclass (max substrate class), .XY2Dnum (count of surveys per grid point)
data_structures.Mop: Mop transect metadata table array with geometric information for all ~11594 Mop points. Fields: .Name, .XYutm (center coords), .BackXutm, .BackYutm (back beach/land point), .OffXutm, .OffYutm (offshore point), .Normal (orientation), .UTMzone. Defines the transect lines used for cross-shore analysis
data_structures.DataSets: Configuration struct array defining file path patterns and source types for different survey data sources (GPS, LiDAR, etc). Used to discover and classify survey files automatically.
